core_mechanism: random-matrix-theory universality in the eigenvalue and eigenvector-component statistics of a large interacting operator, where a symmetry-breaking perturbation drives a crossover from regular/non-ergodic spectral correlations to fully ergodic random-matrix statistics
brief_reason: The abstract diagnoses the many-body Hamiltonian's spectrum via random-matrix universality, a disorder-driven regular-to-chaotic (non-ergodic-to-ergodic) crossover tied to a broken approximate symmetry, Porter-Thomas fluctuation statistics of the eigenvector-derived oscillator strengths, and a Thouless time from long-range spectral correlations.
